methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $85M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $600M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.1x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

private.name: Asimov

private.name: Profluent
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series B), post-money $600M

data.narrative:
Basecamp Research is a Series B biotech company founded by Oliver Vince and Glen Gowers that has raised $85M to date. Valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 8 private-stage biotech companies and 3 public comparables (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) in the Biotech & Health sector.

Two quantitative methods prove applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x multiplier to the $85M total raised, implying a $162M valuation. Peer-median anchoring uses four same-stage biotech companies—Eikon Therapeutics ($3.3B), Neko Health ($1.8B), Asimov ($800M), and Profluent ($600M)—yielding a median valuation of $1.3B and a range of $600M to $3.3B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied due to lack of disclosed revenue for the target.

The triangulated valuation range spans $137M (low) to $1.5B (high), with a median of $731M. The wide spread reflects material divergence between stage-based and peer-median approaches. Stage-progression assumes conservative Series B step-ups relative to capital deployed; peer anchoring captures a broader market distribution where frontier-biotech rounds at this stage command substantial premiums. Key uncertainties include the target's IP position relative to the peer set, capital efficiency, and stage of clinical or product advancement relative to the four peer companies used as anchors.
